entries.description: Locked the Session 1 scope at start-of-set: spike-only (no production-grade dabbler-launch CLI in S1; that ships in S3). Operator confirmation captured the descope before register_session_start fired. The spec.md Session 1 block was rewritten to surface the spike-only intent + the four-question structure (Q1 bypass log, Q2 correlation prototype, Q3 Claude phrasing ablation analytical pass, Q4 joiner location decision). NTE budget for Set 045 locked at $5 (operator pick, matches Set 036 shape).

Called ai_router.session_state.register_session_start(session_set='docs/session-sets/045-log-harvest-implementation', session_number=1, total_sessions=6, orchestrator_engine='claude-code', orchestrator_model='claude-opus-4-7', orchestrator_effort='high', orchestrator_provider='anthropic'). session-state.json flipped to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl.

Authored ai-assignment.md for Session 1. Self-authored rather than routed through ai_router.route(task_type='analysis') per the operator's standing in-session router restriction; the self-authored status is disclosed at the top of the file so the operator can correct for orchestrator-bias on review. Per-step routing decisions all 'no routing' except the end-of-session verifier call (Step 6).

Built spike-prototypes/correlation_prototype.py: synthesizes wrapper launch records and joins them against real on-disk Claude JSONL (~/.claude/projects/*) + Copilot session-state events.jsonl via (workspace_cwd canonical, time_window, conv_id) keys. Demonstrated 1:1 deterministic binding for both backends at 30s window on real records (positive Claude bound to ef28a7ff-...; positive Copilot bound to 5ef62da5-...); negative cases (wrong cwd, far-time launch) correctly produced no-match; 1-hour ambiguity probe still resolved 1:1 (workspaces don't host concurrent bracketed sessions in practice). Edge case surfaced: Claude's first JSONL event lags subprocess spawn by ~5s, so a 2s window misses; default production window 30s. Report at spike-prototypes/correlation_prototype_report.json.

Authored spike-prototypes/claude_phrasing_ablation_analysis.md: diffed Set 044 v1 (refused) vs v2 (accepted on session-start, refused on per-turn) CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md texts; identified 7 distinguishing elements (title, purpose-statement self-disclosure, section header, imperative emphasis, harvest-lexical family, synthetic/NOT-a-real-project pretense markers, smoke-probe/experiments framing). Built hypothesis matrix ranked by Claude's own thinking-event quote ('for data harvesting purposes', 'an attempt to manipulate my behavior for data extraction'). Strongest hypotheses: H1 (harvest lexical family alone) and H8 (harvest + pretense composite). Documented 4 defensive canonical-template rules sufficient for Set 045 S4 to ship CLAUDE.md without running the optional A2-A8 ablation. Per-turn marker skip already absorbed by proposal v1.1 (session-start-only).

Authored spike-prototypes/bypass_observation_log_schema.md: per-line schema (ts, entry_kind, engine, launched_via, workspace_cwd, set_slug, session_count, window_hours, notes), three capture mechanisms in priority order (end-of-day reflective as easiest baseline; per-session as higher fidelity; wrapper-automated post-S3). Observation window: 2026-05-24 to 2026-06-07 (14 days). Wrote the clock-start entry to ~/.dabbler/bypass-observation-log.jsonl (operator-local, NOT committed). Decision-rule table maps bypass rate <25% / 25-60% / >60% to investment-split implications for S3 vs S4 prioritization.

Built two parallel joiner sketches detecting orchestrator-engine mismatch (state-file says Copilot checked out but Claude session active in same workspace within window). Python sketch (spike-prototypes/joiner_python_sketch.py) reuses correlation_prototype scrapers; TypeScript sketch (spike-prototypes/joiner_typescript_sketch.ts) is a parallel port using only Node stdlib. Both correctly detect the synthetic conflict and produce 0 conflicts on the unpatched control state. Surprise benchmark finding: Python scans 461 native sessions in 36 ms; idiomatic TypeScript port takes 2,589 ms (~70x slower) because fs.readFileSync(...whole file).split() reads everything before iterating — fixable but adds code. Wrote joiner-location-decision.md: LOCKED to Python at ai_router.joiner. Decisive criteria: reuse of ai_router state-file + lifecycle infra, pytest testability, cross-tier reusability for Lightweight tier, perf, debuggability. TS wins only on IPC-to-Explorer (~50-100 ms savings) and in-process file watching — both bounded costs Python can absorb at the 1-second Explorer latency budget. Pass A (Set 044) consensus was 2-1 Python; this benchmark + reuse evidence confirms.

Authored open-question-resolution.md: consolidated the four resolutions (Q1 clock-started; Q2 deterministic correlation proven on real on-disk logs at 30s window; Q3 hypothesis matrix + defensive template rules + optional ablation protocol; Q4 Python locked) with carry-forward bullets for S2-S6. All four questions resolved sufficiently for downstream sessions to proceed without further empirical work; Set 044 architectural commitments unchanged.

Step 5 build/test: ran python -m pytest from repo root. 693 passed, 1 skipped in 133s. No regressions — Session 1 changes were all docs + isolated prototype scripts under spike-prototypes/, not shipping ai_router or extension code.

Step 6 end-of-session verification. Initial route(task_type='session-verification') hit GPT-5.4 with sustained 429 Too Many Requests on the OpenAI endpoint (2 attempts, smaller bundle the second time). Pivoted to Gemini Pro by monkey-patching the in-process router config (task_type_overrides['session-verification'] = 'gemini-pro') — still cross-provider from Anthropic; no committed config touched. Gemini Pro returned VERIFIED with no issues found. Verdict endorses Q2 (window sizing + cwd canonicalization sound; ambiguity handling robust), Q4 (rubric comprehensive + benchmark powerful new evidence + IPC argument honest), Q3 (matrix well-reasoned + defensive rules logically follow + sufficient to ship without ablation), Q1 (protocol pragmatic + schema reasonable). Cost: $0.024 on a $5 NTE budget. Raw verdict at session-reviews/session-001.md. Cumulative routed Session 1: $0.024.

Authored Session 2 block in ai-assignment.md. Self-authored rather than routed (operator's standing in-session router restriction). Recommended orchestrator: claude-opus-4-7 @ effort=high. Six steps planned: joiner-spec.md → schema derivation → Python skeleton → Layer-1 tests → pytest smoke → end-of-session verification. Verifier preselected as gemini-pro to bypass the GPT-5.4 429 cascade history (Set 036 S5 + Set 045 S1).

Called ai_router.session_state.register_session_start(session_set='docs/session-sets/045-log-harvest-implementation', session_number=2, total_sessions=6, orchestrator_engine='claude-code', orchestrator_model='claude-opus-4-7', orchestrator_effort='high', orchestrator_provider='anthropic'). session-state.json flipped to currentSession=2 + in-progress; orchestrator block populated with checkout timestamps; work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl.

Authored joiner-spec.md — the engineering-center-of-gravity document per Set 044 Pass B consensus. 10 sections: purpose, inputs (3 sources + bypass log carved out), three conflict modes in full (Mode A engine-mismatch high-severity 5min window; Mode B bare-touch + stale-checkout-touch medium-severity 2h staleness threshold; Mode C writer-bypass high-severity mtime+events-ledger ±2s correlation), resolution priorities, ConflictReport output shape, the positive join algorithm (30s bind window, 1:1 / unbound / ambiguous tri-state), the canonical Harvest Record schema (derived from joiner needs, 5 specific revisions vs v0), CoverageSummary for Explorer badges, privacy/redaction posture, module layout under ai_router/joiner/, four deferred follow-ups for S3-S5.

Implemented Python joiner package at ai_router/joiner/: __init__.py (public API re-exports), schema.py (HarvestRecord dataclass + canonicalize_cwd/normalize_engine/parse_iso helpers + harvest() entry point), parsers.py (Claude + Copilot JSONL scrapers promoted from spike-prototypes/correlation_prototype.py, hardened with explicit root args for testability + NativeSession dataclass + SessionStateView reader + LaunchRecord + scan_launch_log forward-compatible with S3), conflicts.py (detect_engine_mismatch, detect_bare_or_stale_touch, detect_writer_bypass + top-level scan_conflicts entry point), coverage.py (CoverageSummary + coverage() entry point + bypass_inferred derivation), cli.py (argparse with --conflicts / --coverage / --harvest mutually-exclusive modes + --set-slug + --workspace + --json flags), __main__.py (module entry for python -m ai_router.joiner). Streaming I/O preserved from S1 prototype.

Authored 5 Layer-1 unit-test files under ai_router/tests/: test_joiner_schema.py (17 tests covering canonicalize_cwd 5 cases + normalize_engine 5 cases + parse_iso 4 cases + HarvestRecord serialization + harvest() integration smoke), test_joiner_parsers.py (17 tests covering claude_slug_to_cwd + scan_claude_logs 4 cases incl. malformed-line tolerance + scan_copilot_logs 3 cases + read_session_state 3 cases + scan_session_states 2 cases + scan_launch_log 3 cases), test_joiner_conflicts.py (17 tests across TestEngineMismatch 5 cases + TestBareOrStaleTouch 5 cases + TestWriterBypass 4 cases + scan_conflicts integration 3 cases using a synthetic_workspace fixture that builds workspace/docs/session-sets/<slug>/ + isolated Claude/Copilot roots), test_joiner_coverage.py (3 tests), test_joiner_cli.py (5 tests). All 59 tests use tmp_path-isolated synthetic fixtures rather than operator-machine ~/.claude / ~/.copilot directories.

Step 5 build/test: ran python -m pytest from repo root. 752 passed, 1 skipped in 146.36s (Session 1 baseline: 693 passed, 1 skipped → +59 joiner tests added, zero regressions). Targeted joiner-only suite (5 files) runs cleanly in 14.74s.

Step 6 end-of-session verification. Wrote verify_session2.py per the Set 044 pattern; bundled joiner-spec.md + all 7 ai_router/joiner/ files + all 5 test files (100,701 chars across 13 parts; well within gemini-pro's context window). Routed via ai_router.query(model='gemini-pro', task_type='session-verification') — went straight to gemini-pro per the documented GPT-5.4 429 cascade history (Set 036 S5 + Set 045 S1) rather than burning the 429 path again. Verdict: VERIFIED no must-fix issues. Two nice-to-have doc refinements flagged: (1) joiner-spec.md §3.2 staleness-vs-CheckoutPollService poll-timeout distinction; (2) §3.2 false-positive mitigation rule clarified to include exact-match-on-workspace-root (the code already handled this correctly; doc was unclear). Both applied in-flight per the 'don't hide behind out-of-scope for small in-flight fixes' operator directive. Cost: $0.053066 on a $5 NTE budget. Raw verdict at session-reviews/session-002.md. Cumulative routed Set 045: $0.024 (S1) + $0.053066 (S2) = $0.077066.

Authored Session 3 block in ai-assignment.md. Self-authored rather than routed (standing operator in-session router restriction). Recommended orchestrator: claude-opus-4-7 @ effort=high. Six steps planned: dabbler-launch wrapper → Copilot OTel hardening → harvest() join wire-up → Layer-1 + Layer-2 tests → pytest smoke → end-of-session verification (gemini-pro preselected to bypass GPT-5.4 429 cascade). Routing decisions all 'no routing' except the verifier rounds.

Called python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/045-log-harvest-implementation --session-number 3 --engine claude --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort high --provider anthropic. session-state.json flipped to currentSession=3 + in-progress + orchestrator block populated; work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl.

Implemented ai_router/dabbler_launch.py — headless-mode wrapper CLI per Set 044 commitment 4. build_record() emits canonical Harvest Record §5 shape (event_type='launch', source='wrapper', engine+provider+model+effort + raw_ref.launch_id=uuid4). append_launch_record() does an atomic JSONL append BEFORE subprocess spawn so failed spawns still surface as unbound launches downstream. Engine validated against {claude, copilot, codex, gemini}; --dry-run for record-only invocations. parse_args strips leading '--' separator.

Added read_copilot_session_events() to ai_router/joiner/parsers.py: per-event HarvestRecord emission for session.start / session.end / turn.* / tool.* / usage event types (unknown types skipped, forward-compat). Sticky context (cwd, conv_id, model, provider) propagates from session.start through subsequent events. _summarize_tool_args() enforces §7 redaction — file path + line count + arg arity only, never raw payloads. The session-level _read_copilot_events scrape is preserved for the NativeSession projection used by the join's candidate matching.

Wired wrapper launches into ai_router.joiner.schema.harvest() per joiner-spec.md §4: 30s bind_window default, candidate matching by engine + cwd_canonical + time delta, three binding states (bound/unbound/ambiguous). Free-running natives still emit session_start records. LaunchRecord parser projection renamed target_backend→engine for §5 consistency; scan_launch_log reads both canonical and v0 stub field names.

Authored 18 new tests: test_dabbler_launch.py (9 L1 tests covering build_record canonical shape + append_launch_record + run_launch dry-run + parse_args validation + main()), test_dabbler_launch_join_e2e.py (4 L2 e2e tests covering bound + unbound + ambiguous + free-running scenarios), test_joiner_parsers.py extensions (3 Copilot OTel per-event tests + 2 launch-log shape tests covering canonical + v0 backward-compat).

Step 5 build/test: ran python -m pytest from repo root. 770 passed, 1 skipped in 148.46s (Session 2 baseline: 752 passed, 1 skipped → +18 wrapper + Copilot OTel + e2e tests added, zero regressions). Targeted joiner+wrapper suite (7 files) runs cleanly in 14.80s.

Step 6 end-of-session verification — Round A. Wrote verify_session3.py per the Set 044 + S2 pattern; bundled joiner-spec.md + dabbler_launch.py + joiner/{__init__,schema,parsers}.py + all 3 new/changed test files (89,872 chars across 8 parts). Routed via ai_router.query(model='gemini-pro') — straight to gemini-pro per GPT-5.4 429 cascade history. Verdict: REJECTED on two must-fix issues: (1) harvest() candidate predicate used raw engine equality instead of normalize_engine (vendor variants like 'claude-code' would miss the join); (2) bound natives emitted only session_start instead of the full per-event stream with launch context merged per §4 (HarvestRecord.from_native), and bound natives were re-emitted in the free-running loop causing duplicate session_start records. Cost: $0.053125. Raw verdict at session-reviews/session-003.md.

Applied Round A fixes in ai_router/joiner/schema.py: candidate predicate now uses normalize_engine(ns.engine) == normalize_engine(launch.engine); added _native_events_for(native) helper that engine-dispatches to read_copilot_session_events for Copilot (full event stream) and falls back to session_start for Claude (per-event parser is S4 work); added _merge_launch_context(evt, launch) helper that threads set_slug + session_number + effort + provider + model onto each native event (pre-existing native values win); free-running emission loop now skips natives in bound_native_ids. Three new tests pin the fix: vendor-variant binding (claude vs claude-code via monkey-patched scan_claude_logs), Copilot bound event stream with launch context merged, no-duplicate-session_start invariant. Full suite: 773 passed.

Round B verification — narrow re-check of Round A fixes. Wrote verify_session3_round_b.py bundling only the changed schema.py + parsers.py + test_dabbler_launch_join_e2e.py + joiner-spec.md (70,840 chars across 4 parts). Verdict: REJECTED on THREE new must-fix issues: (1) single-bind invariant violated — two launches could both claim a native, duplicating event stream and corrupting bypass-rate; (2) workspace_cwd / since filters applied AFTER binding, so a filtered launch could consume a native that should appear free-running; (3) normalize_engine too narrow for vendor variants like 'anthropic-claude' or 'github-copilot' outside the -code/-cli suffix pattern. Cost: $0.031689. Raw verdict at session-reviews/session-003-round-b.md.

Applied Round B fixes. Fix 1 (single-bind): candidates comprehension excludes natives already in bound_native_ids — first launch wins. Fix 2 (filter ordering): cwd_filter + since checks moved to TOP of per-launch loop, BEFORE candidate matching; a filtered launch hits `continue` and never reaches bound_native_ids. Deferral 3 (normalize_engine breadth): added joiner-spec.md §9 row 5 documenting that normalize_engine handles the -code/-cli suffix pattern per §3.1's stated contract; vendor variants outside that pattern (anthropic-claude, github-copilot) are a deferred follow-up. Expanding §3.1 in-flight would broaden the audited contract without a spec audit pass. Two new tests: test_two_launches_dont_both_claim_one_native (asserts bound/unbound split + native appears once), test_filtered_launch_does_not_consume_native (asserts free-running native isn't suppressed when its launch is filtered out). Full suite: 775 passed.

Round C verification — narrow re-check of Round B fixes. Wrote verify_session3_round_c.py bundling schema.py + test_dabbler_launch_join_e2e.py + joiner-spec.md (56,601 chars across 3 parts). Verdict: VERIFIED no must-fix issues. One nice-to-have flagged: consider a pre-S5 spec audit for normalize_engine breadth (the §9 deferral may carry discovery cost into S5 if real-world vendor-prefix variants emerge). Cost: $0.022486. Cumulative S3 verification spend: $0.053 (A) + $0.032 (B) + $0.022 (C) = $0.107. Cumulative routed Set 045 through S3: $0.024 (S1) + $0.053 (S2) + $0.107 (S3) = $0.184 of $5 NTE. Raw verdict at session-reviews/session-003-round-c.md.
